**Alternate proofs**

- `chapters/theory/higher_genus_modular_koszul.tex:29936-29945` — Part (H) of `thm:five-from-theta` treats Theorem H as a projection of the master MC element. The shadow obstruction tower forces the Hochschild spectral sequence to degenerate at \(E_2\) (`prop:e2-formality-hochschild`), so polynomial growth and the \(\{0,1,2\}\)-concentration follow from no new higher differentials, while Koszul functoriality is the homotopy invariance of the shadow deformation complex (`thm:shadow-homotopy-invariance`). This is a distinct derivation from the one in `chiral_hochschild_koszul.tex`.

- `chapters/theory/higher_genus_foundations.tex:2670-2730` — `cor:hochschild-duality` restates the duality as a Ran-space bar–cobar/Verdier argument, and `rem:hochschild-shift-origin` spells out the configuration-space collapse: Verdier duality on each \(\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)\) supplies a shift of \(p+2\), but Koszul concentration (`thm:bar-concentration`) forces the surviving summands to lie on the diagonal \(p=q\), so the variable shift collapses to the constant \([2]\). That remark is a direct explanation of how the configuration-space geometry feeds into the duality shift.

The other chapters you mentioned (`chapters/theory/hochschild_cohomology.tex`, `chapters/theory/chiral_center_theorem.tex`, etc.) currently cite Theorem H/`thm:main-koszul-hoch` rather than re-proving it, so the two surfaces above are the only alternative derivations and the one that explicates the configuration-space collapse.

graded dual exists, we write $\mathcal{A}^! := C_{\mathcal{A}}^\vee$
for the corresponding dual algebra. Then the bar-cobar adjunction
restricts to an equivalence of derived categories between

where:
\begin{itemize}
\item $\mathcal{V}^* = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{V}, \omega_X)$ is the dual shifted by the canonical bundle
\item $R^{\perp}$ consists of relations orthogonal to $R$ under the canonical pairing
\[
\langle \cdot, \cdot \rangle: j_*j^*(\mathcal{V}^* \boxtimes \mathcal{V}) \to j_*\omega_{X^2 \setminus \Delta}
\]
\end{itemize}
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
\end{remark}

\begin{example}[Explicit correspondence for Heisenberg]
For the Heisenberg chiral algebra $\mathcal{H}_k$ at level~$k$ with generator $\alpha(z)$ and OPE:
\[\alpha(z)\alpha(w) \sim \frac{k}{(z-w)^2}\]

The Koszul dual $\mathcal{H}^!$ has:

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)

+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).

+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Hochschild cohomology by the bar-cobar resolution

+\simeq

+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). +The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+is the homotopy Koszul dual factorization algebra of~$\cA$.
+Passing from the ordered bar to the symmetric Hochschild object is
+exactly passage to totalized $\Sigma_n$-coinvariants in each bar
+degree, so
+\[
+\bigl((\cA)^!_\infty\bigr)_{\Sigma}
+\cong
+\bigl(\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)_{\Sigma}.
+\]
+
+On the Koszul locus, Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration} identifies
+the bar coalgebra with its bar cohomology coalgebra
+$\cA^i = H^*(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA))$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on the curve gives
+$\cA^! = \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i)$, and Theorem~A identifies the resulting
+factorization algebra with $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+Thus the bar coalgebra, its cohomology coalgebra, and the Koszul dual
+algebra remain separated by the chain
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\to \cA^i \xrightarrow{\mathbb{D}_X}\cA^!,
+\]
+which is the comparison used in Theorem~H.
+
+Finally, Theorem~\ref{thm:hochschild-bar-cobar} computes chiral
+Hochschild cohomology by the bar-cobar resolution
+\[
+\ChirHoch^\bullet(\cA)
+\simeq
+R\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{ChirAlg}}
+\bigl(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)),\cA\bigr),
+\]
+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). +The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on the curve gives

+algebra remain separated by the chain
+\[

+which is the comparison used in Theorem~H.

+Hochschild cohomology by the bar-cobar resolution
+\[
+\ChirHoch^\bullet(\cA)
+\simeq
+R\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{ChirAlg}}
+\bigl(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)),\cA\bigr),
+\]
+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

\subsection{The universal twisting morphism}

The universal twisting morphism
$\pi\colon \barB_X(\cA) \to \cA$
(Proposition~\ref{prop:universal-twisting-adjunction}) controls
all bar-cobar identifications in this chapter. Twisting morphisms
are MC elements in the convolution algebra $\mathrm{Hom}(C, A)$
(Definition~\ref{def:twisting-morphism}). The natural bijection
$\mathrm{Tw}(C, A) \cong \mathrm{Hom}(C, B(A)) \cong
\mathrm{Hom}(\Omega(C), A)$
mediates the bar-cobar adjunction
(Proposition~\ref{prop:universal-twisting-adjunction}(iii)); for the
geometric realization as a propagator integral, see
Remark~\ref{rem:twisting-as-propagator}.

\begin{example}[Twisting for fermion-boson duality]
\label{ex:fermion-boson-twisting}
For the Koszul pair (free fermions $\mathcal{F}$, $\beta\gamma$
system), the universal twisting morphism
 succeeded in 52ms:
\]
where:
\begin{itemize}
\item $\mathcal{V}^* = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{V}, \omega_X)$ is the dual shifted by the canonical bundle
\item $R^{\perp}$ consists of relations orthogonal to $R$ under the canonical pairing
\[
\langle \cdot, \cdot \rangle: j_*j^*(\mathcal{V}^* \boxtimes \mathcal{V}) \to j_*\omega_{X^2 \setminus \Delta}
\]
\end{itemize}
\end{definition}

Its bar cohomology
\[
\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
\]
is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}

+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)

+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).

+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$

+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i

+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

**Considering dimensions and contributions**

I'm pondering whether infinitely many "p" contribute to a given situation, which might suggest dimensions could also be infinite. But do we actually know that only a finite number of "p" contribute? Current proof doesn’t clarify that. So I wonder if the spectral sequence and totalization might indicate every "p" contributes equally, suggesting it could really be an infinite sum. This makes our claim about finiteness seem possibly unsupported.

**Evaluating the theorem's structure**

I think I should remove the sentence about each \(\ChirHoch^n\) being finite-dimensional unless I can justify the finite sum. The theorem already uses dimensions, so I'm questioning how to justify that. The audit suggested a finite-dimensionality hypothesis or a reformulation. The user is focused on the Hilbert series of polynomial growth in relation to the theorem. To avoid unsupported claims, perhaps I can remove that sentence while keeping the theorem statement unchanged, but that leaves some assumptions in place.
thinking
**Revisiting the theorem details**

I’m considering rephrasing part (b) to say, "the Hilbert series in the Grothendieck group is a polynomial," but that changes the statement. The user didn't raise this issue, so maybe I shouldn't widen the scope. However, if the theorem relies on unsupported dimensions, that's a big problem. The spectral sequence is finite for each total degree because of the filtration's length, but what if the total degree shifts? It could mean infinite dimensions without an extra finite-type assumption. The theorem likely aims for generic examples with finite Hochschild cohomology.

I’m wondering if all chiral Koszul algebras in this context have only finitely many strong generators, leading to finite Hochschild cohomology. But I can't be sure of that. I should check if an earlier theorem or definition indicates that "chiral Koszul algebra" implies finite generation. Maybe the definition includes that, so some searching might help. The statement uses "chiral Koszul datum," which might suggest finite generation is inherently present, but I'm not completely confident. I'll also look into whether the definitions mention anything about finite generation.

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by
+collision forests; the closed filtration by collision codimension
+induces~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}.
+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products
+of a coherent $\mathcal{D}_X$-module on the base curve with the
+logarithmic de~Rham complex of a product of local model fibers
+$\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$.
+The local complex on the fiber is exactly the bar complex governing
+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.
+
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}, the local bar cohomology is
+concentrated in bar-differential degree~$0$.
+Writing
+\[
+\cA^i_p := H^{p,0}\!\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr),
+\]
+the associated graded of the collision-depth filtration is therefore
+quasi-isomorphic to a complex on the base curve with coefficient
+object~$\cA^i_p$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on~$X$ gives the
+degree-$p$ piece of the Koszul dual algebra,
 \[
-H^j(Y_p, \mathcal{M})

+which yields the $E_2$-page description~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-e2}.
 
-In the total complex, the $p$-th summand is placed in degree~$p$
-by the shift~$[-p]$, so the combined grading contribution at

+The higher differentials vanish for either of two standard reasons.
+First, the local fibers $\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$ are formal for
+$m\geq 2$ by the configuration-space formality package
+\textup{(}Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:en-formality}}\textup{)},
+so after passing to fiber cohomology there is no remaining
+chain-level room for $d_m$ with $m\geq 2$.
+Equivalent phrasing: the same strata carry pure mixed Hodge
+structures, and Deligne strictness forbids a nonzero
+weight-changing differential beyond~$E_2$.
+Thus $E_2(p)=E_\infty(p)$.

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)

+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).

+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.

+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products

 \end{proof}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by
+collision forests; the closed filtration by collision codimension
+induces~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}.
+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products
+of a coherent $\mathcal{D}_X$-module on the base curve with the
+logarithmic de~Rham complex of a product of local model fibers
+$\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$.
+The local complex on the fiber is exactly the bar complex governing
+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.
+
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}, the local bar cohomology is
+concentrated in bar-differential degree~$0$.
+Writing
+\[
+\cA^i_p := H^{p,0}\!\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr),
+\]
+the associated graded of the collision-depth filtration is therefore
+quasi-isomorphic to a complex on the base curve with coefficient
+object~$\cA^i_p$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on~$X$ gives the
+degree-$p$ piece of the Koszul dual algebra,
 \[
-H^j(Y_p, \mathcal{M})
-\;\cong\;
-H^{d_p - j}(Y_p, \mathbb{D}\mathcal{M})^\vee,

+\simeq

+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+$m\geq 2$ by the configuration-space formality package

+so after passing to fiber cohomology there is no remaining
+chain-level room for $d_m$ with $m\geq 2$.
+Equivalent phrasing: the same strata carry pure mixed Hodge
+structures, and Deligne strictness forbids a nonzero
+weight-changing differential beyond~$E_2$.
+Thus $E_2(p)=E_\infty(p)$.

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by
+collision forests; the closed filtration by collision codimension
+induces~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}.
+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products
+of a coherent $\mathcal{D}_X$-module on the base curve with the
+logarithmic de~Rham complex of a product of local model fibers
+$\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$.
+The local complex on the fiber is exactly the bar complex governing
+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.
+
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}, the local bar cohomology is
+concentrated in bar-differential degree~$0$.
+Writing
+\[
+\cA^i_p := H^{p,0}\!\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr),
+\]
+the associated graded of the collision-depth filtration is therefore
+quasi-isomorphic to a complex on the base curve with coefficient

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by
+collision forests; the closed filtration by collision codimension
+induces~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}.
+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products
+of a coherent $\mathcal{D}_X$-module on the base curve with the
+logarithmic de~Rham complex of a product of local model fibers
+$\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$.
+The local complex on the fiber is exactly the bar complex governing
+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.
+
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}, the local bar cohomology is
+concentrated in bar-differential degree~$0$.
+Writing
+\[
+\cA^i_p := H^{p,0}\!\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr),
+\]
+the associated graded of the collision-depth filtration is therefore
+quasi-isomorphic to a complex on the base curve with coefficient
+object~$\cA^i_p$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on~$X$ gives the
+degree-$p$ piece of the Koszul dual algebra,
 \[
-H^j(Y_p, \mathcal{M})

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by
+collision forests; the closed filtration by collision codimension
+induces~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}.
+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products
+of a coherent $\mathcal{D}_X$-module on the base curve with the
+logarithmic de~Rham complex of a product of local model fibers
+$\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$.
+The local complex on the fiber is exactly the bar complex governing
+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.
+
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}, the local bar cohomology is
+concentrated in bar-differential degree~$0$.

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. +The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by
+collision forests; the closed filtration by collision codimension
+induces~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}.
+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products
+of a coherent $\mathcal{D}_X$-module on the base curve with the
+logarithmic de~Rham complex of a product of local model fibers
+$\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$.
+The local complex on the fiber is exactly the bar complex governing
+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.
+
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}, the local bar cohomology is
+concentrated in bar-differential degree~$0$.

+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(3),

+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+curve~$X$, and for each bar degree~$p$, let

+gives a first-quadrant spectral sequence

+On the Koszul locus this spectral sequence has:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
-\item \emph{Verdier shift:} $[p+2]$, from
- $\dim_{\mathbb{C}} \overline{C}_{p+2}(X) = p + 2$
- \textup{(}the FM compactification of $p+2$ points on a curve
- has complex dimension $p+2$\textup{)};
-\item \emph{Totalization shift:} $[-p]$, from the convention
- $RHH_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA) := \mathrm{Tot}\bigl(
- \bigoplus_{p \geq 0} CH^{p,\bullet}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)[-p]\bigr)$;
-\item \emph{Net shift:} $(p+2) + (-p) = 2$, independent of~$p$.
+\item \emph{Curve-level $E_2$ page:}
+ \begin{equation}\label{eq:hoch-fm-e2}
+ E_2^{r,s}(p)=0 \text{ for } s\neq p,
+ \qquad
+ E_2^{r,p}(p)\cong
+ \Ext_{\mathcal{D}_X}^r\!\bigl((\cA^!)_p,\omega_X\bigr).
+ \end{equation}
+\item \emph{$E_2$-degeneration:} all higher differentials

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}

The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. +The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 succeeded in 52ms:
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Part~(a): fix a bar degree~$p$.
Lemma~\ref{lem:hochschild-shift-computation} furnishes a
first-quadrant spectral sequence
\[
E_2^{r,s}(p)=0 \text{ for } s\neq p,
\qquad
E_2^{r,p}(p)\cong
\Ext_{\mathcal{D}_X}^r\!\bigl((\cA^!)_p,\omega_X\bigr),
\]
and this spectral sequence degenerates at~$E_2$.
Because $X$ is a smooth projective curve,
\[
\Ext_{\mathcal{D}_X}^r\!\bigl((\cA^!)_p,\omega_X\bigr)=0
\qquad\text{for } r\notin\{0,1,2\},
\]
so every bar-degree summand of the Hochschild total complex has
cohomological amplitude~$[0,2]$.

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+\operatorname{gr}_{F_p}^{\,r}
+CH^{p,\bullet}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\Bigr)
+\;\Longrightarrow\;
+H^{r+s}\!\bigl(CH^{p,\bullet}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr).
+\end{equation}
+On the Koszul locus this spectral sequence has:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
-\item \emph{Verdier shift:} $[p+2]$, from
- $\dim_{\mathbb{C}} \overline{C}_{p+2}(X) = p + 2$
- \textup{(}the FM compactification of $p+2$ points on a curve
- has complex dimension $p+2$\textup{)};
-\item \emph{Totalization shift:} $[-p]$, from the convention
- $RHH_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA) := \mathrm{Tot}\bigl(
- \bigoplus_{p \geq 0} CH^{p,\bullet}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)[-p]\bigr)$;
-\item \emph{Net shift:} $(p+2) + (-p) = 2$, independent of~$p$.
+\item \emph{Curve-level $E_2$ page:}
+ \begin{equation}\label{eq:hoch-fm-e2}
+ E_2^{r,s}(p)=0 \text{ for } s\neq p,
+ \qquad
+ E_2^{r,p}(p)\cong
+ \Ext_{\mathcal{D}_X}^r\!\bigl((\cA^!)_p,\omega_X\bigr).
+ \end{equation}
+\item \emph{$E_2$-degeneration:} all higher differentials
+ $d_m$ with $m\geq 2$ vanish. Equivalently,
+ $E_2(p)=E_\infty(p)$.
+\item \emph{Uniform shift:} the geometric Verdier shift
+ $[p+2]$ from $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ and the totalization shift
+ $[-p]$ from
+ $RHH_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA) :=
+ \operatorname{Tot}(\bigoplus_{p \geq 0}
+ CH^{p,\bullet}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)[-p])$
+ combine to the constant shift~$[2]$.
+\item \emph{Amplitude bound:} because $\dim_{\mathbb{C}} X = 1$,
+ \[
+ \Ext_{\mathcal{D}_X}^r\!\bigl((\cA^!)_p,\omega_X\bigr)=0
+ \qquad\text{for } r\notin\{0,1,2\}.
+ \]
 \end{enumerate}
-Koszul concentration
-\textup{(}Theorem~\textup{\ref{thm:bar-concentration})}
-forces $H^{p,q} = 0$ for $q \neq 0$, so only the diagonal $p = q$
-survives. The variable bar-degree index~$p$ thereby drops out of
-the final answer, and the Verdier duality shift collapses to the
-\emph{uniform constant}~$[2]$. The remaining $\omega_X$ twist
-arises from $\mathcal{D}$-module duality on the base curve:
-the dualizing object for right $\mathcal{D}_X$-modules is~$\omega_X$.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}
-At bar degree~$p$, the Hochschild complex lives on the smooth proper
-variety $Y_p := \overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ of complex dimension
-$d_p = p + 2$. Serre--Verdier duality for holonomic
-$\mathcal{D}$-modules on~$Y_p$ gives
+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by
+collision forests; the closed filtration by collision codimension
+induces~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}.
+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products
+of a coherent $\mathcal{D}_X$-module on the base curve with the
+logarithmic de~Rham complex of a product of local model fibers
+$\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$.
+The local complex on the fiber is exactly the bar complex governing
+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.
+
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}, the local bar cohomology is
+concentrated in bar-differential degree~$0$.
+Writing
 \[
-H^j(Y_p, \mathcal{M})
-\;\cong\;
-H^{d_p - j}(Y_p, \mathbb{D}\mathcal{M})^\vee,
+\cA^i_p := H^{p,0}\!\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr),
+\]
+the associated graded of the collision-depth filtration is therefore
+quasi-isomorphic to a complex on the base curve with coefficient
+object~$\cA^i_p$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on~$X$ gives the
+degree-$p$ piece of the Koszul dual algebra,
+\[
+(\cA^!)_p = \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i_p),
 \]
-so the duality on the $p$-th bar summand carries a shift of~$[d_p] = [p+2]$.
+which yields the $E_2$-page description~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-e2}.
 
-In the total complex, the $p$-th summand is placed in degree~$p$
-by the shift~$[-p]$, so the combined grading contribution at
-bar degree~$p$ is $(p+2) - p = 2$.
+The higher differentials vanish for either of two standard reasons.
+First, the local fibers $\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$ are formal for
+$m\geq 2$ by the configuration-space formality package
+\textup{(}Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:en-formality}}\textup{)},
+so after passing to fiber cohomology there is no remaining
+chain-level room for $d_m$ with $m\geq 2$.
+Equivalent phrasing: the same strata carry pure mixed Hodge
+structures, and Deligne strictness forbids a nonzero
+weight-changing differential beyond~$E_2$.
+Thus $E_2(p)=E_\infty(p)$.

+For the shift, Serre--Verdier duality on the smooth proper variety
+$\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ contributes $[p+2]$, since
+$\dim_{\mathbb{C}}\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)=p+2$.
+The totalization of the bigraded Hochschild complex contributes
+$[-p]$, so the net shift on every surviving $E_\infty$-term is

-The $\omega_X$ factor: Serre duality on the one-dimensional base

-This twist is intrinsic to $\mathcal{D}$-module duality on~$X$ and
-independent of the configuration-space geometry.
+Finally, on a smooth complex curve the global Ext functor for
+holonomic $\mathcal{D}_X$-modules has amplitude~$[0,2]$.
+Hence the only nonzero columns on the $E_2$-page are
+$r=0,1,2$, proving the amplitude statement.
 \end{proof}

 \label{lem:chirhoch-descent}
 \index{chiral Hochschild!descent from bar}

+Concretely, on the Koszul locus
 \textup{(}Definition~\textup{\ref{def:chiral-koszul-morphism})}
 there is a natural identification of chain complexes
 \begin{equation}
 \label{eq:chirhoch-descent}
 \ChirHoch^{\bullet}(\cA)
 \;\cong\;
+\bigl((\cA)^!_\infty\bigr)_{\Sigma}
+\;\cong\;
 \bigl(\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)_{\Sigma},
 \end{equation}
 where $(-)_{\Sigma}$ denotes totalized $\Sigma_n$-coinvariants in
 bar degree~$n$.
+If
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr),
+\qquad
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i),
+\]
+then this is the corrected comparison chain

+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,

+with no direct identification of the bar coalgebra itself with

 \end{lemma}

-the dual coalgebra image of the bar complex; passage from the

-$\bar{B}^{\Sigma}(\cA)$ is by $\Sigma_n$-coinvariants in each bar
-degree. On the Koszul locus, Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}
-collapses $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ to its cohomological
-diagonal; applying $\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}$ and taking totalized
-$\Sigma$-coinvariants identifies the result with the chiral
-Hochschild complex computed via bar-cobar resolution
-$\mathrm{Hom}(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)), \cA)$.
-The bar-cobar quasi-isomorphism

+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(3),
+the Verdier dual bar object
+\[
+(\cA)^!_\infty
+=
+\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}_X(\cA)
+\]
+is the homotopy Koszul dual factorization algebra of~$\cA$.
+Passing from the ordered bar to the symmetric Hochschild object is
+exactly passage to totalized $\Sigma_n$-coinvariants in each bar
+degree, so
+\[
+\bigl((\cA)^!_\infty\bigr)_{\Sigma}
+\cong
+\bigl(\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)_{\Sigma}.
+\]
+
+On the Koszul locus, Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration} identifies
+the bar coalgebra with its bar cohomology coalgebra
+$\cA^i = H^*(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA))$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on the curve gives
+$\cA^! = \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i)$, and Theorem~A identifies the resulting
+factorization algebra with $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+Thus the bar coalgebra, its cohomology coalgebra, and the Koszul dual
+algebra remain separated by the chain
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\to \cA^i \xrightarrow{\mathbb{D}_X}\cA^!,
+\]
+which is the comparison used in Theorem~H.
+
+Finally, Theorem~\ref{thm:hochschild-bar-cobar} computes chiral
+Hochschild cohomology by the bar-cobar resolution
+\[
+\ChirHoch^\bullet(\cA)
+\simeq
+R\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{ChirAlg}}
+\bigl(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)),\cA\bigr),
+\]
+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

 \subsection{Main duality theorem}\label{subsec:hochschild-duality}
 
-\begin{lemma}[Hochschild duality shift; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
+\begin{lemma}[Fulton--MacPherson collapse and Hochschild duality
+shift; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{lem:hochschild-shift-computation}
 \index{Hochschild cohomology!duality shift computation}
 For a chiral Koszul pair $(\cA, \cA^!)$ on a smooth projective
-curve~$X$ of genus~$g$, the Verdier duality shift in the bigraded
-chiral Hochschild complex
+curve~$X$, and for each bar degree~$p$, let
+$F_p^\bullet \overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ denote the
+Fulton--MacPherson collision-depth filtration by closed unions of
+strata of codimension at least the filtration index.
+The induced filtration on the bigraded chiral Hochschild complex
 \textup{(}Definition~\textup{\ref{def:bigraded-hochschild})}
-collapses to a uniform constant. More precisely, at bar degree~$p$
-the three grading contributions are:
+gives a first-quadrant spectral sequence
+\begin{equation}\label{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}
+E_1^{r,s}(p)
+\;=\;
+H^{r+s}\!\Bigl(
+\operatorname{gr}_{F_p}^{\,r}
+CH^{p,\bullet}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\Bigr)
+\;\Longrightarrow\;
+H^{r+s}\!\bigl(CH^{p,\bullet}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr).
+\end{equation}
+On the Koszul locus this spectral sequence has:
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\roman*)}]
-\item \emph{Verdier shift:} $[p+2]$, from
- $\dim_{\mathbb{C}} \overline{C}_{p+2}(X) = p + 2$
- \textup{(}the FM compactification of $p+2$ points on a curve
- has complex dimension $p+2$\textup{)};
-\item \emph{Totalization shift:} $[-p]$, from the convention
- $RHH_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA) := \mathrm{Tot}\bigl(
- \bigoplus_{p \geq 0} CH^{p,\bullet}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)[-p]\bigr)$;
-\item \emph{Net shift:} $(p+2) + (-p) = 2$, independent of~$p$.
+\item \emph{Curve-level $E_2$ page:}
+ \begin{equation}\label{eq:hoch-fm-e2}
+ E_2^{r,s}(p)=0 \text{ for } s\neq p,
+ \qquad
+ E_2^{r,p}(p)\cong
+ \Ext_{\mathcal{D}_X}^r\!\bigl((\cA^!)_p,\omega_X\bigr).
+ \end{equation}
+\item \emph{$E_2$-degeneration:} all higher differentials
+ $d_m$ with $m\geq 2$ vanish. Equivalently,
+ $E_2(p)=E_\infty(p)$.
+\item \emph{Uniform shift:} the geometric Verdier shift
+ $[p+2]$ from $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ and the totalization shift
+ $[-p]$ from
+ $RHH_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA) :=
+ \operatorname{Tot}(\bigoplus_{p \geq 0}
+ CH^{p,\bullet}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)[-p])$
+ combine to the constant shift~$[2]$.
+\item \emph{Amplitude bound:} because $\dim_{\mathbb{C}} X = 1$,
+ \[
+ \Ext_{\mathcal{D}_X}^r\!\bigl((\cA^!)_p,\omega_X\bigr)=0
+ \qquad\text{for } r\notin\{0,1,2\}.
+ \]
 \end{enumerate}

-the final answer, and the Verdier duality shift collapses to the
-\emph{uniform constant}~$[2]$. The remaining $\omega_X$ twist
-arises from $\mathcal{D}$-module duality on the base curve:
-the dualizing object for right $\mathcal{D}_X$-modules is~$\omega_X$.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}
-At bar degree~$p$, the Hochschild complex lives on the smooth proper
-variety $Y_p := \overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ of complex dimension
-$d_p = p + 2$. Serre--Verdier duality for holonomic

+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by
+collision forests; the closed filtration by collision codimension
+induces~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}.
+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products
+of a coherent $\mathcal{D}_X$-module on the base curve with the
+logarithmic de~Rham complex of a product of local model fibers
+$\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$.
+The local complex on the fiber is exactly the bar complex governing
+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.
+
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}, the local bar cohomology is
+concentrated in bar-differential degree~$0$.
+Writing
 \[
-H^j(Y_p, \mathcal{M})
-\;\cong\;
-H^{d_p - j}(Y_p, \mathbb{D}\mathcal{M})^\vee,
+\cA^i_p := H^{p,0}\!\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr),
+\]
+the associated graded of the collision-depth filtration is therefore
+quasi-isomorphic to a complex on the base curve with coefficient
+object~$\cA^i_p$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on~$X$ gives the
+degree-$p$ piece of the Koszul dual algebra,
+\[
+(\cA^!)_p = \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i_p),

+The higher differentials vanish for either of two standard reasons.
+First, the local fibers $\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$ are formal for
+$m\geq 2$ by the configuration-space formality package
+\textup{(}Proposition~\textup{\ref{prop:en-formality}}\textup{)},
+so after passing to fiber cohomology there is no remaining
+chain-level room for $d_m$ with $m\geq 2$.
+Equivalent phrasing: the same strata carry pure mixed Hodge
+structures, and Deligne strictness forbids a nonzero
+weight-changing differential beyond~$E_2$.
+Thus $E_2(p)=E_\infty(p)$.

+$\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ contributes $[p+2]$, since

+Finally, on a smooth complex curve the global Ext functor for
+holonomic $\mathcal{D}_X$-modules has amplitude~$[0,2]$.
+Hence the only nonzero columns on the $E_2$-page are
+$r=0,1,2$, proving the amplitude statement.
 \end{proof}

+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(3),
+the Verdier dual bar object
+\[
+(\cA)^!_\infty
+=
+\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}_X(\cA)

+is the homotopy Koszul dual factorization algebra of~$\cA$.
+Passing from the ordered bar to the symmetric Hochschild object is

+degree, so

+\bigl((\cA)^!_\infty\bigr)_{\Sigma}
+\cong

+On the Koszul locus, Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration} identifies
+the bar coalgebra with its bar cohomology coalgebra
+$\cA^i = H^*(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA))$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on the curve gives
+$\cA^! = \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i)$, and Theorem~A identifies the resulting
+factorization algebra with $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+Thus the bar coalgebra, its cohomology coalgebra, and the Koszul dual
+algebra remain separated by the chain
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\to \cA^i \xrightarrow{\mathbb{D}_X}\cA^!,
+\]
+which is the comparison used in Theorem~H.
+
+Finally, Theorem~\ref{thm:hochschild-bar-cobar} computes chiral
+Hochschild cohomology by the bar-cobar resolution
+\[

+\simeq
+R\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{ChirAlg}}
+\bigl(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)),\cA\bigr),
+\]
+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated

+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by

+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(3),

+\[
+(\cA)^!_\infty

+\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}_X(\cA)

+factorization algebra with $(\cA)^!_\infty$.

+algebra remain separated by the chain
+\[

+which is the comparison used in Theorem~H.

+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}

+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. +The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{proof}
-Part~(a): by Theorem~\ref{thm:hochschild-bar-cobar},
+Part~(a): fix a bar degree~$p$.
+Lemma~\ref{lem:hochschild-shift-computation} furnishes a
+first-quadrant spectral sequence
 \[
-\ChirHoch^n(\cA)
-\;=\;
-\mathrm{Ext}^n_{\mathrm{ChirAlg}(X)}(\cA, \cA),
+E_2^{r,s}(p)=0 \text{ for } s\neq p,
+\qquad
+E_2^{r,p}(p)\cong
+\Ext_{\mathcal{D}_X}^r\!\bigl((\cA^!)_p,\omega_X\bigr),
 \]
-so $\ChirHoch^n(\cA)=0$ for $n<0$. For $n>2$,
-Theorem~\ref{thm:main-koszul-hoch} gives
+and this spectral sequence degenerates at~$E_2$.
+Because $X$ is a smooth projective curve,
 \[
-\ChirHoch^n(\cA)
-\;\cong\;
-\ChirHoch^{2-n}(\cA^!)^\vee \otimes \omega_X.
+\Ext_{\mathcal{D}_X}^r\!\bigl((\cA^!)_p,\omega_X\bigr)=0
+\qquad\text{for } r\notin\{0,1,2\},
 \]
-Since $2-n<0$, the right-hand side vanishes. Hence
-$\ChirHoch^n(\cA)=0$ for $n>2$, and the only possible
-nonzero degrees are $0$, $1$, and~$2$.
+so every bar-degree summand of the Hochschild total complex has
+cohomological amplitude~$[0,2]$.
+The totalization is again first-quadrant, hence
+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA)=0$ for $n<0$, and the amplitude bound gives
+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA)=0$ for $n>2$.
+Thus the only possible nonzero degrees are $0$, $1$, and~$2$.
 
 Part~(b): part~(a) implies that $P_\cA(t)$ is a polynomial of
 degree at most~$2$. Its constant term is

exec

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by
+collision forests; the closed filtration by collision codimension
+induces~\eqref{eq:hoch-fm-filtration}.
+Each graded piece is a finite direct sum of external tensor products
+of a coherent $\mathcal{D}_X$-module on the base curve with the
+logarithmic de~Rham complex of a product of local model fibers
+$\FM_m(\mathbb{C})$.
+The local complex on the fiber is exactly the bar complex governing
+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.
+
+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration}, the local bar cohomology is
+concentrated in bar-differential degree~$0$.

+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(3),
+the Verdier dual bar object
+\[
+(\cA)^!_\infty
+=
+\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}_X(\cA)
+\]
+is the homotopy Koszul dual factorization algebra of~$\cA$.
+Passing from the ordered bar to the symmetric Hochschild object is
+exactly passage to totalized $\Sigma_n$-coinvariants in each bar
+degree, so
+\[
+\bigl((\cA)^!_\infty\bigr)_{\Sigma}
+\cong
+\bigl(\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)_{\Sigma}.
+\]
+
+On the Koszul locus, Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration} identifies
+the bar coalgebra with its bar cohomology coalgebra
+$\cA^i = H^*(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA))$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on the curve gives
+$\cA^! = \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i)$, and Theorem~A identifies the resulting
+factorization algebra with $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+Thus the bar coalgebra, its cohomology coalgebra, and the Koszul dual
+algebra remain separated by the chain
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\to \cA^i \xrightarrow{\mathbb{D}_X}\cA^!,
+\]
+which is the comparison used in Theorem~H.
+
+Finally, Theorem~\ref{thm:hochschild-bar-cobar} computes chiral
+Hochschild cohomology by the bar-cobar resolution
+\[
+\ChirHoch^\bullet(\cA)
+\simeq
+R\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{ChirAlg}}
+\bigl(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)),\cA\bigr),
+\]
+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

**Evaluating build error**

It looks like the build error is due to aux corruption rather than our patch. I might need to rerun the build after cleaning things up. The main.aux seems to have some NUL bytes, so I should classify this as a stale artifact. Maybe I can run `make clean` or remove aux altogether. Since the build failed because of the corrupted aux, not because of the code itself, running `make clean` followed by `make fast` seems like a solid plan. I'll mention that and use exec.
codex
The fast build failed before TeX reached the edited theorem lane: `main.aux` is corrupted with embedded NUL bytes, so `pdflatex` aborts on pass 1. I’m restabilizing the build surface with a clean rebuild to separate artifact noise from actual manuscript errors.

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Fix~$p$.
+The FM compactification $\overline{C}_{p+2}(X)$ is stratified by

+iterated OPE residues at the corresponding collision pattern.

+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(3),
+the Verdier dual bar object
+\[
+(\cA)^!_\infty
+=
+\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}_X(\cA)
+\]
+is the homotopy Koszul dual factorization algebra of~$\cA$.
+Passing from the ordered bar to the symmetric Hochschild object is
+exactly passage to totalized $\Sigma_n$-coinvariants in each bar
+degree, so
+\[
+\bigl((\cA)^!_\infty\bigr)_{\Sigma}
+\cong
+\bigl(\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)_{\Sigma}.
+\]
+
+On the Koszul locus, Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration} identifies
+the bar coalgebra with its bar cohomology coalgebra
+$\cA^i = H^*(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA))$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on the curve gives
+$\cA^! = \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i)$, and Theorem~A identifies the resulting
+factorization algebra with $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+Thus the bar coalgebra, its cohomology coalgebra, and the Koszul dual
+algebra remain separated by the chain
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\to \cA^i \xrightarrow{\mathbb{D}_X}\cA^!,
+\]
+which is the comparison used in Theorem~H.
+
+Finally, Theorem~\ref{thm:hochschild-bar-cobar} computes chiral
+Hochschild cohomology by the bar-cobar resolution
+\[
+\ChirHoch^\bullet(\cA)
+\simeq
+R\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{ChirAlg}}
+\bigl(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)),\cA\bigr),
+\]
+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

+cohomological amplitude~$[0,2]$.
+The totalization is again first-quadrant, hence
+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA)=0$ for $n<0$, and the amplitude bound gives
+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA)=0$ for $n>2$.
+Thus the only possible nonzero degrees are $0$, $1$, and~$2$.

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA)=0$ for $n<0$, and the amplitude bound gives
+$\ChirHoch^n(\cA)=0$ for $n>2$.
+Thus the only possible nonzero degrees are $0$, $1$, and~$2$.
 
 Part~(b): part~(a) implies that $P_\cA(t)$ is a polynomial of

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). +The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

+Thus the only possible nonzero degrees are $0$, $1$, and~$2$.
 
 Part~(b): part~(a) implies that $P_\cA(t)$ is a polynomial of

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). +The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). +The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(3),
+the Verdier dual bar object
+\[
+(\cA)^!_\infty
+=
+\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}_X(\cA)

+is the homotopy Koszul dual factorization algebra of~$\cA$.
+Passing from the ordered bar to the symmetric Hochschild object is

+degree, so
+\[
+\bigl((\cA)^!_\infty\bigr)_{\Sigma}
+\cong

+On the Koszul locus, Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-concentration} identifies
+the bar coalgebra with its bar cohomology coalgebra
+$\cA^i = H^*(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA))$.
+Applying degreewise Verdier duality on the curve gives
+$\cA^! = \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i)$, and Theorem~A identifies the resulting
+factorization algebra with $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+Thus the bar coalgebra, its cohomology coalgebra, and the Koszul dual
+algebra remain separated by the chain
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\to \cA^i \xrightarrow{\mathbb{D}_X}\cA^!,
+\]
+which is the comparison used in Theorem~H.
+
+Finally, Theorem~\ref{thm:hochschild-bar-cobar} computes chiral
+Hochschild cohomology by the bar-cobar resolution
+\[

+\simeq
+R\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{ChirAlg}}
+\bigl(\Omega^{\mathrm{ch}}(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)),\cA\bigr),
+\]
+and the bar-cobar counit from
+Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(1) identifies this
+with the $\Sigma$-coinvariant descent of $(\cA)^!_\infty$.
+This gives~\eqref{eq:chirhoch-descent}.
 \end{proof}

thinking
**Considering build script options**

I'm thinking about possibly building a script that runs quietly. I wonder if it would be best to set the polling interval to around 30 seconds. That might help balance efficiency and resource usage, but I have to consider whether that's too frequent or infrequent for what I'm trying to accomplish. I'll weigh the pros and cons a bit more to ensure it's the right approach.

The clean rebuild is still running. The first failure was definitely stale-aux noise; I’m waiting for the stabilized pass to see whether any real theorem-lane errors remain once TeX starts from a clean state.

 \begin{remark}[Three objects]\label{rem:koszul-dual-meaning}
-The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is its graded linear dual: $\cA^! = \bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)^\vee$. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$ itself: $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}). What is a relation in~$\cA$ becomes cofreedom in~$\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ and freedom in~$\cA^!$.
+The bar construction $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)$ is the
+bar \emph{coalgebra}: a conilpotent dg coalgebra cogenerated
+by~$\mathcal{V}^*$ with corelations~$R^{\perp}$.
+Its bar cohomology
+\[
+\cA^i := H^*\bigl(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)\bigr)
+\]
+is the dual \emph{coalgebra}. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}
+is obtained only after Verdier duality on the curve:
+\[
+\cA^! := \mathbb{D}_X(\cA^i).
+\]
+Only on finite-type lanes does this reduce to an ordinary
+graded linear dual. The cobar of the bar recovers~$\cA$
+itself:
+$\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+\textup{(}Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-inversion-qi}\textup{)}.
+Thus the corrected comparison chain is
+\[
+\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
+\;\longrightarrow\;
+\cA^i
+\;\xrightarrow{\ \mathbb{D}_X\ }\;
+\cA^!,
+\]
+with inversion $\Omega(\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)) \simeq \cA$
+separate from Koszul duality.
 \end{remark}

+Fix~$p$.

+By Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}(3),
+the Verdier dual bar object
+\[
+(\cA)^!_\infty
+=
+\mathbb{D}_{\Ran}\bar{B}_X(\cA)
